w:r.w:t: You now have a factory full of isolated machines calculating hyper-specific math (Clashes, Biology, Volatile Economies). But without
, they are just engines sitting in a dark room.
that moves the data between them, and the
Here are the developer-ready blueprints for the final two modules.
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 08: The Campaign & World State Manager
A stateful Python microservice backed by a database. While every other module we built forgets what happens the moment the math is done,
It holds the master save file, tracks the passing of time, tracks active quests, and remembers that a player burned down a tavern in Node 45.
SQLite or PostgreSQL (using SQLAlchemy for strict data relations).
├── schemas.py # JSON payloads for VTT syncing
├── time_manager.py # Advances the calendar and triggers events
└── quest_tracker.py # Logic gates for objective completion
This is the payload the Game Master app requests when a player logs into the VTT.
# Drops a pin on the Macro Map
# Overrides the original C++ Master World Generation
When players take a "Long Rest" or Travel, time advances. This engine checks if Quests expire or if Factions make a move.
# 2. Check for Quest Timeouts (e.g., "Save the merchant before dawn")

When a player clicks a new hexagon on the Azgaar map, this module calculates the travel time based on the C++ Edges data, subtracts Rations from the inventory, and asks the Encounter Manager (Module 4) if they get ambushed along the road.
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 09: The Game Master App (The API Gateway)
The React VTT does not talk to any other module. It only talks to the GM App.
The GM App receives a player's intent, acts as the API Gateway to route the requests to Modules 1-8, collects the math, and feeds it into the
httpx (To fire off API calls to the other local microservices instantly).
├── server.py # The single endpoint the VTT talks to
├── vtt_schemas.py # The unified Input/Output for the React App
├── api_gateway.py # The httpx client that talks to Modules 1-8
└── prompts.py # The strict instructions for the LLM
This is the universal translator between the UI and the Math.
# "I swing my heavy greatsword at the wolf's neck!"
# Tactical grit from the UI slider
# The beautiful story text to display in chat
# "[SYSTEM: Scrape! Wolf took 4 dmg. Kaelen lost 2 Stamina.]"
# Tells React to fetch new HP/Stamina bars
This is the most important piece of code in the entire project. This is how the GM App ties the whole factory together seamlessly.
# Send the math to the Clash Engine (Module 7)
We use LangGraph to ensure the AI
makes up the rules. It must wait for the API Gateway to return the math first.
# 1. Parse Intent (What is the player doing?)
# 2. Get Character & Enemy Stats (via Gateway to Modules 3 & 4)
# 3. Roll the Dice (via Gateway to Module 7 - Clash Engine)
# 4. Feed the raw math to the LLM Narrator
Write a 2-sentence gritty narration of this specific outcome.
Do NOT invent any extra damage or effects.
Take a step back and look at what we have architected. You have successfully designed a modular, AAA-grade TTRPG backend.
If you boot up all 9 modules, here is the exact lifecycle of a single click on your VTT:
Receives the request. Routes it to the Item Foundry.
Sends the "Heal 3 Stamina" command to the Character Creator.
Updates the player's 12-stat Character Sheet JSON to add 3 Stamina.
Sends the "Success: 3 Stamina Recovered" to the AI Narrator.
"You pop the cork and inhale deeply. The sharp, metallic sting of the salts clears the fog from your mind, revitalizing your muscles."
Displays the text and physically animates the Stamina Bar increasing by 3.
The True Power of this Architecture:
cheat. It doesn't have access to the HP variables; it only has access to the text box.
Want to add a new class of weapons? You don't have to touch the VTT, the AI, or the Database. You just add a JSON entry to
and the factory handles the rest.
You now have a complete, 9-part Master Design Document. You can take any of these 9 blueprints, hand it to an AI coder (like Claude 3.5 or Cursor), and say:
Are you ready to start building the first box on the factory line?
